summary: Completed iteration 3 testing of TimeSheet dashboard balances, work location selector, 7 leave types, and comprehensive navigation. Backend: 16/16 tests passed. Frontend: All pages load and function correctly, but 'Mes soldes' section and work location selector are NOT rendering in the dashboard UI despite the code being present.

ui_bugs.issue: 'Mes soldes' section (balances) is NOT rendering even though API returns valid data and code exists at lines 483-523
Work location selector (Lieu: Bureau/Teletravail/Chantier) is NOT rendering when project is selected, despite code at lines 340-352
data-testid attributes are not being rendered to DOM (React Native Web issue). Elements like clock-section, project-select-section return null in DOM queries

issues: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'boxShadow' - cosmetic only

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix 'Mes soldes' section not rendering - balances state might be null or conditional rendering logic failing
CRITICAL: Fix work location selector not rendering when project is selected - check preSelectedProjectId state
Investigate why React Native Web is not passing data-testid attributes to DOM
The code at index.tsx lines 340-352 (work location) and 483-523 (balances) exists but is not rendering
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard index.tsx: Conditional rendering {balances && ...} at line 484 suggests balances state might be null - check if getBalances() API call is succeeding and setBalances(balRes.data) is being executed

Dashboard index.tsx: Work location selector at line 341 has condition {preSelectedProjectId && ...} - when project chip is clicked and turns blue, the state SHOULD be set but UI doesn't show the selector
Possible React state update issue - the visual feedback (project chip highlighted, Arrivee button green) suggests state is updating but child components aren't re-rendering

success_rate.frontend: 85% - All navigation works, but dashboard balances/location selector not rendering

seed_data_creation: Created 7 leave types via backend tests (TEST_vacation_leave, TEST_sick_leave, etc.), 1 expense (TEST_expense)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs all working: /api/stats/balances returns vacation/overtime/telework data, /api/timeentries/clock-in accepts work_location parameter. Frontend issue: Dashboard code exists for 'Mes soldes' section (lines 483-523) and work location selector (lines 340-352) in index.tsx, but they are NOT rendering to the DOM. All other pages work: Absences (7 leave types visible in modal), Planning (weekly grid with department filter), Expenses, Directory, Reports, Notifications, Audit logs, Invoices.

frontend.dashboard_weekly_stats: PASS - Shows Heures/semaine, Facturables, Supplementaires, Jours travailles
frontend.dashboard_mes_soldes: FAIL - NOT RENDERING despite code existing
frontend.dashboard_work_location_selector: FAIL - NOT RENDERING when project selected

rca_of_the_issue.issue: 'Mes soldes' section and work location selector not rendering
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause_hypothesis: The React component state for 'balances' might be null (API call failing silently or data not being set) OR there's a React Native Web rendering issue where the conditional {balances && ...} block is not re-rendering after state update
evidence: API /api/stats/balances returns valid data with all fields
Code exists at index.tsx lines 483-523 for Mes soldes
Code exists at index.tsx lines 340-352 for work location selector
Project selection works (chip turns blue, Arrivee button turns green)
Weekly stats section renders correctly (uses similar pattern)
mitigation_steps: Add console.log to check if balances state is being set after API call
Check if there are any errors in the browser console during data loading
Verify the loadData() function is completing successfully
Consider adding a loading state or fallback for the balances section
